title: Local presence/transport daemon (eliminate the per-session holder)
summary: Design foundation in progress: replace the per-session holder with an auto-spawned per-user daemon (presence + transport), plus the Copilot plugin (sessionEnd hook + skill) and seamless upgrade, so idle long-lived sessions stay wakeable and stations stop going stale.

nodes.title: Design foundation: daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, upgrade
nodes.summary: Make the daemon architecture explicit and validated: DECISIONS ADR(s), DESIGN.md station->daemon update, PRODUCT-THESIS.md no-server->local-daemon update, the IPC/attendance protocol, the liveness model (hook healthy-path + a generic, typed --watch-pid pid-watch backstop, no idle-TTL teardown) plus minimal stale-attendance (last-confirmed + occupied_stale + takeover, no teardown), the lease-epoch fencing token (epoch-guarded heartbeat/release; self-demote on a 0-row heartbeat), the daemon singleton identity (user SID + config root + protocol-major), durable-buffer reuse of 0011/0013, and the epoch-based upgrade/handoff design, the server-side epoch fence on delivery emission + ordered handoff, the seen-dedup redesign for a long-lived daemon, the daemon lifecycle contract + Status surface, the daemon-scoped capability/version-handshake IPC, daemon-native session RPCs (Register/Re-register/DeregisterSession), the takeover state algebra, and the verb/docs cutover. Resolves the open implementation-design questions.

nodes.title: Design accepted
nodes.summary: Builder validates the daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, and upgrade design before any implementation proceeds. Spar + council critique resolved.

nodes.title: Daemon core + one-shot verbs (SQLite)
nodes.summary: Auto-spawned per-user single-instance daemon (singleton keyed by user SID + config root + protocol-major) owning the SQLite poll loop, durable buffer (reuse 0011/0013 deliveries), daemon-scoped IPC (Register/Re-register/Deregister/DeregisterSession/Wait/Status; frames carry the lease epoch), attendance registry (in-memory session->addresses + attendance_last_confirmed_at staleness), daemon-written epoch-guarded lease heartbeat (self-demote on a 0-row heartbeat), server-side epoch-gated delivery emission (mark_delivered_if_current_owner; no Message frame unless the daemon owns the epoch), the redesigned bounded/durable dedup (the seen invariant no longer relies on holder restart), and a generic typed --watch-pid pid-watch backstop (v1 floor: loader anchor + start-time; harness-agnostic). Refactor attach/detach into one-shot register/deregister and wait into a daemon client, so a session runs zero persistent telex processes. Includes the minimal upgrade floor (versioned shim + daemon stop --drain + next-call respawn + legacy-holder/non-epoch-lease cutover rule) and the daemon lifecycle contract (OS spawn-lock, connect-or-spawn, readiness ACK, wait reconnect-on-EOF grace, exit codes, Status surface); acceptance includes the four lifecycle tests (concurrent first-use, crash-during-wait, competing daemons, handoff duplicates). With the Copilot plugin this is the first operator-unblocking slice: holder/waiter race + orphans + turn-loop starvation gone on SQLite.

nodes.title: Fencing proof (epoch-guarded emission + ordered handoff)
nodes.summary: Executable proof that delivery emission is epoch-gated server-side and that graceful handoff/crash cannot double-deliver or flip ownership, on SQLite first. Distinct gate that postgres-parity, copilot-plugin, and seamless-upgrade depend on; downstream branches must not proceed until it passes.

nodes.title: Postgres parity
nodes.summary: One LISTEN/NOTIFY connection in the daemon; daemon heartbeats local attendees' backend leases; per-machine presence with the backend lease as cross-machine source of truth. The daemon-respawn reclaim race is handled by epoch fencing; this node must prove epoch behavior under competing daemons/machines before it lands. Sketch; detailed at promotion.

nodes.title: Copilot plugin: sessionEnd hook + skill
nodes.summary: sessionEnd hook calls a daemon-native DeregisterSession(session_id, proof) RPC (healthy disconnect, quit + dismiss) - the daemon's in-memory session->addresses map is the authority, NOT PR #31's filesystem registry (its hook plumbing is reused, the registry storage is dropped). The hook is a thin mapper of the Copilot env/payload (COPILOT_AGENT_SESSION_ID -> TELEX_SESSION_ID; COPILOT_LOADER_PID -> --watch-pid) onto generic inputs; the core COPILOT_* fallback is removed so Copilot JSON parsing is not a core protocol dependency. Move telex skill into a real Copilot plugin skill with one source serving both the CLI command and the plugin skill. Sketch; may split hook vs skill at promotion.

nodes.title: Seamless upgrade (#6)
nodes.summary: Versioned install + stable launcher shim + telex upgrade/rollback/gc + daemon drain-restart/handoff (epoch-based), so upgrades never require tearing down stations. Floor (fencing-ready schema + minimal restart) already lands in daemon-core; this node adds the full upgrade platform LAST, after Postgres + plugin are proven, so it never blocks the operator unblock. Sketch; detailed at promotion.

nodes.title: End-to-end unblock + closure
nodes.summary: Builder validates the full system: give a real session the new plugin + new telex and confirm it is unblocked end-to-end. Retire superseded mechanisms (pid-watch-as-holder, #3 relay, the re-arm dance) and update SKILL.md.

checkpoints.title: Design foundation aligned
checkpoints.summary: Daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, durable-buffer reuse, and upgrade design are explicit and builder-validated.

checkpoints.title: Daemon core usable (SQLite)
checkpoints.summary: A CLI session attends and receives through the always-on daemon with zero per-session telex processes; SQLite path proven.

checkpoints.title: SQLite unblock shipped
checkpoints.summary: First operator-unblocking slice: daemon-core + fencing-proof + Copilot plugin + minimal upgrade floor on SQLite - a real session can take the new plugin + telex and be unblocked.

checkpoints.title: Both backends + plugin + upgrade complete
checkpoints.summary: Postgres parity, the Copilot plugin (hook + skill), and seamless upgrade all landed.

checkpoints.title: Closure ready
checkpoints.summary: End-to-end unblock validated; superseded mechanisms and docs retired/updated.
